summary: Docker buildx build failed when wget could not download the nydus release binary from GitHub due to an HTTP 502 Bad Gateway response from the server.
root_cause: The wget command attempting to download https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz received an HTTP 502 Bad Gateway error from the remote server, causing the process to exit with code 1.

evidence.quote: wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: Direct evidence that the remote server returned a 502 error, a transient server-side error

evidence.quote: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: Buildx build directly failed due to the wget command exiting with code 1 from the 502 error

evidence.quote: ##[error]buildx bake failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: GitHub Actions error confirming buildx bake failure is directly caused by the wget 502 error

suggested_fix: Retry the build. The HTTP 502 Bad Gateway is a transient server-side error. If failures persist, verify network connectivity to github.com and the dragonflyoss/nydus releases endpoint.

summary: Single functional test failed due to network error: test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub] encountered repeated HTTP 502 responses from github.com when attempting to download a test package archive, exhausting retry attempts. All other 2833 tests passed.
root_cause: HTTPSConnectionPool connection to github.com exhausted max retries after receiving multiple HTTP 502 Bad Gateway error responses when downloading https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip

evidence.quote: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: Explicit error message indicating GitHub returned HTTP 502 Bad Gateway errors repeatedly until the retry limit was exhausted

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: Error message repeated 4 times in output, showing consistent network failure rather than random test flakiness
Only 1 failure out of 2833 total tests (1753 unit tests all passed, 1080 of 1081 functional tests passed), indicating isolated network issue, not a code defect

suggested_fix: Retry the CI job. The failure is due to temporary GitHub service unavailability (HTTP 502 errors). This is transient and should resolve when GitHub recovers or on the next CI run.

summary: One jest test failed out of 485 total tests. The specific failing test and assertion details are not visible in the log excerpt, but the test suite completed with exit code 1 indicating test assertion failure.
root_cause: A single jest test assertion failed during the test run. The test suite summary shows '1 failed, 9 skipped, 475 passed, 485 total', and the jest command exited with code 1.

evidence.quote: Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
evidence.reason: Explicitly shows one test suite failed among 65 total suites
Shows one individual test failed among 485 total tests
Indicates the jest command for the test script failed, halting the pnpm recursive run
Confirms jest exited with code 1, indicating test failure rather than execution/setup error

suggested_fix: Review the specific failing test output to identify which test assertion failed and why. The full jest output should show the failing test name and the assertion error message.
